title: phaca polaris
intro: Phaca polaris, also known as the Arctic lupine, is a perennial plant native to the Arctic tundra. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its high content of alkaloids and glycosides. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and inflammation, while also exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Phaca polaris is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried extract to harness its medicinal benefits, although further research is required to fully understand its potential applications.
